- open gemini 3.0 pro

The "how does AI improve individual productivity" discussion is much less interesting than "how does AI improve organizational efficiency?" We have a lot of answers to the former, but it will always get eaten by the latter if we don't experiment with new approaches to organizing.

Microsoft seems to be launching its own branded version of Cowork (though I hesitate to discuss products I haven’t tried)

A big question is whether it will continue to use lower-end models without telling you. Also whether it will keep up as the space evolves, or is it a one-off

To me, the question of whether Microsoft will give access to the most recent/best models is big:  Given that GPT-5 beat or tied humans in expert tasks (GDPval) less than 38% of the time while months later, GPT-5.4 beat or tied human experts 82% of the time, this really matters.

Another big question: is this limited to producing materials that use Microsoft Apps? How does it handle the fact that so much of what makes Claude Cowork interesting is the fact that it can improvise all sorts of output using code.

just realized most people running openclaw automations are hosting their workflows on their own machine.

cron jobs on a macbook. scheduled tasks on a laptop that goes to sleep. webhook listeners that die when you close the lid.

this is how agencies lose clients.

the client's
The absolute Goat of marketing and there isnt even a debate about it.

your AI is building n8n workflows blind.

doesn't matter if you're using cursor, claude code, chatgpt, or openclaw. without synta MCP, your AI is:

→ guessing node names (and getting them wrong)
→ hallucinating parameters that don't exist
→ missing community nodes entirely
→

the reason most n8n workflows break in production isn't the logic.

it's a wrong field name. a misconfigured auth. a parameter that worked in testing but fails on real data.

and you don't find out until a client asks why their automation stopped working 3 weeks ago.

synta fixes

you have to ship and fix as you go to win
even Apple’s Appstore connect has bugs
